Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Standard-of-care (SOC) plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily and prednisolone 5 mg daily (SOC + AAP)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.reasoning: The abstract states: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer." It further clarifies under the 'Abstract' and 'Introduction' sections that SOC was androgen deprivation therapy. The 'Results' section explicitly states: "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression." The 'Procedures' section (2.3) confirms: "Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)."

Treatment Arm - N.reasoning: In the '3 | RESULTS' section, it states: 'Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group) and 501 (50%) to standard treatment plus abiraterone and prednisolone (SOC + AAP group) (Figure 1).' The experimental arm is the 'SOC + AAP group', which had 501 participants.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.reasoning: The title of the article states "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy". The abstract further clarifies that patients were randomized to "SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)". Abiraterone acetate is an androgen biosynthesis inhibitor, and prednisolone is a corticosteroid. Therefore, the class of agent in the experimental arm (Treatment Arm 1) is an androgen biosynthesis inhibitor and corticosteroid.
